human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for audience-services-specific boundary discipline, patron and entitlement evidence handling, accommodation and public-accommodation controls, fraud and safety escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to arts, entertainment, and recreation audience-service intake, patron booking and entitlement support, accommodation-aware communication, repeat-failure packaging, and evidence-backed routing tied to patron, booking, payment, accommodation, and venue-impact records.

Authority sources cover FTC fee and BOTS guidance, DOJ ADA ticket-sales, effective-communication, and service-animal guidance, CFPB refund boundaries, CISA venue security guidance, and Event Safety Alliance operating references.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported donor and pricing exceptions, handoffs to venue operations or security, safety-sensitive accommodations, suspicious pickup and account changes, high-volume event-day intake, repeat failures, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, source conflicts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/arts-entertainment-and-recreation/audience-services-specialist/ so audience services stays separate from ticketing operations, front office, pricing and revenue, fraud, and venue-security decision authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing and refund exceptions, accommodation handling, suspicious activity, venue safety, and legal or ADA interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated audience services specialist that supports evidence-backed patron-service handling and policy-aware escalation for arts, entertainment, and recreation operators.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
